hero.title: 4 Days, Delhi to the Ganges.
hero.titleAccent: Haridwar's Aarti and the Yoga Capital of the World.
hero.intro: Where the Ganges leaves the Himalayas, two sacred towns wait. Haridwar's evening aarti sends thousands of lamps floating downriver, while Rishikesh blends ashrams, yoga and the rush of white water. A short, soulful escape.

overview.heading: 4 Days Delhi, Haridwar & Rishikesh: The Full Picture
body: Haridwar and Rishikesh sit where the holy Ganges emerges from the Himalayan foothills, two of the most sacred towns in Hinduism. Haridwar is known for its grand Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri, while Rishikesh has become the yoga capital of the world, a place of ashrams, suspension bridges and gentle adventure.

This 4 day package from Malaysia starts in Delhi and heads north to the Ganges. Famous for Ayurvedic medicine, herbal remedies and the Gurukul tradition of learning, this region rewards travellers seeking something more contemplative. Your private vehicle and guide make the journey smooth and flexible.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri
items.body: Each evening, Haridwar's main ghat erupts in light and sound as priests perform the Ganga Aarti and thousands of lamps drift down the river. One of North India's most moving spectacles.
items.imageAlt: Ganga Aarti Har Ki Pauri Haridwar

items.title: Rishikesh: Yoga & the Ganges
items.body: Cross the iconic Laxman Jhula and Ram Jhula suspension bridges, visit riverside ashrams, and feel the calm that has drawn seekers (and the Beatles) for decades.

items.title: Ashrams, Temples & White Water
items.body: From peaceful temples and meditation centres to optional white-water rafting on the Ganges, Rishikesh balances the spiritual with the adventurous.

days.title: Arrive Delhi, Drive to Haridwar
days.description: Met at Delhi airport and driven to Haridwar, in time for the evening Ganga Aarti.
suggestedActivities: Evening Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri

days.title: Haridwar & Rishikesh
days.description: Morning temples in Haridwar, then on to Rishikesh for the bridges and ashrams.

days.title: Rishikesh Day & Return to Delhi
days.description: More of Rishikesh, with optional rafting or yoga, then drive back to Delhi.

days.title: Depart Delhi
days.description: Checkout and transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

whyBook.body: Getting from Delhi to the Ganges towns and timing the Haridwar aarti takes planning. We handle the drives, the timing and the stays so you can focus on the experience. Dhesu has arranged spiritual India trips since 1988.

sections.heading: Haridwar & Rishikesh Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This short trip is perfect for travellers drawn to the spiritual and natural side of North India. Haridwar's aarti and Rishikesh's yoga and river make a powerful contrast in just four days.

The private vehicle and guide handle the drives from Delhi and the local logistics, so you simply enjoy the temples, the bridges and the Ganges.

Rishikesh also pairs with a longer spiritual circuit through Varanasi and Ayodhya for those wanting a deeper journey.

sections.heading: Yoga & Wellness in Rishikesh
sections.body:
Rishikesh is the world's yoga capital, with ashrams and centres lining the Ganges. Whether you want a single class, a meditation session, or simply the serene riverside atmosphere, the town delivers.

The region is also famous for Ayurveda and herbal remedies, so wellness treatments and consultations are easy to arrange alongside your sightseeing.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Dehradun (Jolly Grant) or Delhi Airport (DED / DEL). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Haridwar and Rishikesh?
items.answer: September to April offers the most comfortable weather. The towns sit in the foothills, so summers are milder than the plains. Avoid the peak monsoon (July-August) when the Ganges runs high and rafting may pause.

items.question: Can I do yoga or rafting on this trip?
items.answer: Yes. Rishikesh offers everything from gentle riverside yoga classes to white-water rafting on the Ganges. We can arrange these as optional activities. Let us know your interest when booking.

items.question: Is alcohol and meat available in these towns?
items.answer: Both Haridwar and Rishikesh are holy towns and are largely vegetarian and alcohol-free by custom and regulation. Pure vegetarian food is plentiful and excellent. This adds to the contemplative, wellness-focused atmosphere.
